free_text: Production  of  medium-weight isotopes by fragmentation
in 750 A MeV 238U on 208Pb collision
Heavy-ion synchrotron.
Fragment separator.
1.25 gr/cm2 lead target.
Two position-sensitive plastic scintillators
located in the intermediate and in the final focal
planes, respectively, provide the time-of-flight and
magnetic rigidity measurements.
The identification in mass and charge is based
on the measurements of the magnetic rigidity, time of
flight and energy loss for each fragment passing
through the fragment separator.
1. Main correction is due to the transmission of the
fragments through the separator. The transmission was
estimated by ion-optical calculations.
2. Correction for dead time of data acquisition which
varied from 5% to 20%, depending on the counting rate.
3. Losses of fragments by secondary reactions in the
target and the intermediate-focus plastic scintillator
are corrected for.
4. All fragments were fully stripped and contributions
from other ionic charge states were negligible.
The results were compared with empirical formula
(EPAX).
Effective proton evaporation barrier was determined.
Empirical
(EPAX) formula.
From Web-site of the laboratory.
No any information.
Corrected data from Website are included
last checking has been done.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. Numerical
data revised. Subents 002-275  merged. PART-DET,
COMMENT deleted. Units in EN changed (GEV -> MEV/A).
In Subents 002-123 the fission portion
of the cross section of the same products is given
SD: SF4=Zn-64 -> ELEM/MASS; SF9=EXP deleted
from REACTION codes. Data from Subents 003-275 added.
